items.title: Cloudflare shipped per-Worker tiered caching
items.oneliner: Cloudflare gave every Worker its own tiered cache. Server-rendered apps can now cut CPU bills without custom cache plumbing.

items.title: Agents-A1 bets on longer task horizons
items.oneliner: Agents-A1 claims 35B parameters can rival much larger models by training on long action traces, not just bigger pretraining.

items.title: Clean code helps coding agents work cheaper
items.oneliner: A controlled study found clean repos cut agent token use 7 to 8 percent and file revisits 34 percent.

items.title: Meta admits agent progress is slower
items.oneliner: Meta says agent development is slower than expected, which is a useful reality check for every roadmap built on autonomy.

items.title: Git config crawlers are actively probing
items.oneliner: Attackers are scraping exposed .git/config files in the wild, so leaked repo metadata is still an easy initial foothold.

items.title: File order can hide covert data
items.oneliner: Researchers showed metadata and code ordering can smuggle hidden payloads, which gives defenders a new class of steganography.

items.title: Cloudflare added AI bot permission controls
items.oneliner: Cloudflare now lets sites classify and gate search, agent, and training bots separately, turning scraping policy into infrastructure.

items.title: MDN got a serious web security refresh
items.oneliner: MDN's new security docs now cover attacks, defenses, threat modeling, and auth, which should raise the floor for web teams.

items.title: Linux 7 benchmark panic needs context
items.oneliner: The Linux 7 Postgres slowdown looks real but benchmark-specific, so most teams should investigate configs before freezing kernel upgrades.

items.title: Rayfish launched a serverless mesh VPN
items.oneliner: Rayfish launched a peer-to-peer VPN with no central operator, pushing private networking closer to product reality.
